title: ranunculus trichophyllus subsp. rionii
intro: Ranunculus trichophyllus subsp. rionii is a subspecies of the lesser celandine plant, also known as buttercup, which is a perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat skin conditions and wounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus trichophyllus subsp. rionii include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a decoction or tincture for use in herbal medicine, although it is essential to note that its use should be under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional due to potential toxicity.
